title: HIPPO Threat Interaction Web
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Explore the five HIPPO threats to biodiversity and analyze how they interact using species case studies.

educational.topic: HIPPO Framework and Threats to Biodiversity
learningObjectives: Differentiate between the five HIPPO threats and analyze how they interact to amplify species vulnerability
Apply the HIPPO framework to specific endangered species case studies
Evaluate which threats are most significant for different species and ecosystems
Explain why addressing biodiversity loss requires targeting multiple interacting threats
prerequisites: Familiarity with the concept of endangered species
Knowledge of habitat loss and its causes

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Case study analysis with interactive network visualization
keyQuestions: Which HIPPO threat affects the greatest number of endangered species?
How do the five threats interact to compound species vulnerability?
If you could eliminate one HIPPO threat globally, which would have the greatest impact on biodiversity conservation?
Why is it insufficient to address only a single threat when trying to save an endangered species?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think of HIPPO threats as independent rather than interacting and compounding
Many students rank overexploitation (hunting/fishing) as the top threat when habitat loss is actually the leading cause of biodiversity loss globally
Students may believe that addressing one threat is sufficient to save a species
assessmentOpportunities: Evaluate species comparison tables for completeness and accuracy of threat identification
Assess conservation action plans for addressing multiple interacting threats
Have students explain causal pathways between threats using evidence from the simulation
